abstract_slice: As autotrophic photosynthetic micro-organisms with the ability to fix nitrogen and carbon into the soil, nitro-gen-fixing cyanobacteria belong to an important microbial resource. The first report on their application in agriculture came from India in 1939. Although it subsequently had global attention, large-scale popularization and application of nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria were not implemented in the end. The reasons were related to the wholesome use of chemical fertilizers in the world and the immature application technology of nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria with high complexity, high cost and low re-liability. For the former, although chemical fertilizers promoted crop production dramatically, its negative effects on agricul-ture and the eco-environment were already a growing concern in the public thinking. abstract_slice: The global agricultural sector faces increasing pressure to enhance productivity while ensuring environmental sustainability. Microalgae-based biofertilizers offer a promising alternative to synthetic fertilizers, leveraging their nutrient-rich profiles and biostimulant properties to improve crop growth, yield, and soil health. This meta-analysis synthesizes findings from 25 studies focusing exclusively on eukaryotic microalgae published over the last 15 years, assessing the effectiveness of microalgae-based biofertilizers across diverse agricultural systems. abstract_slice: Microalgae-derived biofertilizers offer a sustainable alternative to chemical inputs. This study optimized Chlorella vulgaris biomass production and evaluated its efficacy as a biofertilizer for Mung bean (Vigna radiata). A parametric analysis investigated nutrient source, concentration, pH, and light intensity. Results indicated that chicken manure was the superior nutrient source, outperforming goat manure by 181% in biomass yield. The optimal cultivation conditions were identified as 10% (v/v) chicken manure, pH 3.0 +/- 0.5,...
